title: Time Integration Methods for CFD
learning_objectives: Understand the mathematical derivation of temporal discretization schemes from Taylor series
Compare accuracy, stability, and boundedness of Euler, Backward, and Crank-Nicolson schemes
Apply CFL condition for time step selection in transient simulations
Select appropriate time schemes for VOF-based two-phase flow with R410A evaporation

sections.title: Part 1: Core Theory - From Taylor Series to Time Schemes

content.title: Taylor Series Expansion for Time Derivatives
key_points: Forward difference (explicit): truncate Taylor series backward
Backward difference (implicit): truncate Taylor series forward
Central difference: average of forward and backward
Truncation error determines order of accuracy

content.title: Euler Implicit Scheme
key_points: Only needs current and previous time levels

content.title: Backward Differencing Scheme
key_points: Uses three time levels: n+1, n, n-1
Startup requires Euler for first step

content.title: Crank-Nicolson Scheme
key_points: Trapezoidal rule: average of explicit and implicit
Can be bounded with flux limiting (MULES)

sections.title: Part 2: Physical Challenge - Stability in Practice
Part 3: Architecture & Implementation - OpenFOAM ddtScheme Framework

content.title: ddtScheme Class Hierarchy
key_points: Virtual fvmDdt() for implicit matrix assembly

sections.title: Part 4: Quality Assurance - Verification & Best Practices

content.title: Common Issues and Solutions
key_points: Divergence with large Δt → reduce CFL or switch scheme
Mass conservation drift → check temporal accuracy
Startup instability → use Euler for first steps

exercises.question: Derive the backward differencing formula from Taylor series expansion around tⁿ⁺¹
solution_points: Write Taylor series for φⁿ and φⁿ⁻¹
Solve the system of two equations
Show the coefficients match OpenFOAM implementation

exercises.question: Given a velocity of 5 m/s and mesh size 1 mm, calculate the maximum stable Δt for explicit Euler
Compare Euler implicit and Backward schemes for R410A evaporation. Which is preferred and why?
solution_points: Recommendation depends on VOF boundedness requirements

exercises.question: Explain the role of old-old-time fields (V00, phi00) in the backward scheme
solution_points: Storage of second previous time level

exercises.question: A VOF simulation shows interface smearing. Current settings: Euler, Co=0.5. What changes?
Design a verification test for temporal accuracy of the backward scheme
solution_points: Calculate order of accuracy from error reduction

r410a_connections: Time step selection affects VOF interface sharpness
Phase change source terms require stable time integration
Large density ratio (ρ_l/ρ_v ≈ 100 for R410A) affects stability
CFL constraint based on liquid velocity (higher than vapor)
